content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: She ___ the heavy shopping bags from the car to the kitchen door.
distractor_rationale: dragged means pulling something along the ground, but bags are carried by hand from a car
dropped means letting something fall accidentally, which is the opposite of the action described
delivered means bringing goods professionally to an address, not moving your own bags at home

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Tom ___ the window because it was very cold and windy outside.
distractor_rationale: broke means to damage something so it no longer works, not to shut it against the cold
filled means to put something into a container until it is full, which cannot apply to a window
cut means to divide something with a sharp tool, which makes no sense with a window in this context

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The child ___ the ball with both hands before it hit the ground.
distractor_rationale: dropped means letting something fall, which is the opposite of stopping it before it hits the ground
burned means to damage something with heat or fire, which is impossible with a thrown ball
dragged means pulling something along a surface, not stopping an object in mid-air

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: He used a sharp knife to ___ the rope into two equal pieces for the tent.
distractor_rationale: break means to split something by force without a tool, and you cannot break a rope into equal measured pieces with a knife
bend means to curve something without separating it, so it would not produce two pieces
dig means to move earth or soil, which cannot be done to a rope with a knife

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Maria ___ the seat belt tightly before the driver started the car.
distractor_rationale: filled means to put liquid or objects into a container, which cannot be done to a seat belt
dragged means pulling something heavy along a surface, not securing a safety strap
caught means to grab a moving object, but a seat belt is stationary and must be clicked into place

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The gardener used a spade to ___ a deep hole for the new tree in the backyard.
distractor_rationale: cut means to divide something with a blade, not to make a hole in the ground with a spade
drag means to pull something along a surface, not to move earth downward to create a hole
break means to damage something by force, not to systematically remove soil with a spade

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: She ___ the heavy sofa across the floor to move it closer to the window.
distractor_rationale: carried means lifting something off the ground and transporting it, but a heavy sofa is moved by pulling it along the floor, not lifting it
delivered means professionally bringing goods to a location, not moving furniture inside your own home
caught means grabbing a moving object, but a sofa is stationary and is pulled, not grabbed mid-air

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The courier had to ___ thirty packages to different offices in the building before noon.
distractor_rationale: carry means to transport something physically but does not include the professional act of handing items to specific recipients at specific addresses
drop means to let something fall accidentally, which is the opposite of the intended action
bring means to take something to a place but lacks the formal professional sense of a courier completing a scheduled delivery to multiple recipients

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: He had to ___ the metal pipe slightly so that it would fit around the corner of the wall.
distractor_rationale: break means to split or damage the pipe completely, but the sentence says it needed to fit around a corner, requiring only a curve
cut means to divide the pipe into separate pieces with a blade, which would not help it go around a corner
dig means to move earth or soil, which is completely unrelated to shaping a metal pipe

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Anna ___ the jug with cold water from the tap before placing it on the table for her guests.
distractor_rationale: carried means to transport the jug from one place to another, but the sentence specifies adding cold water from the tap, which is filling
closed means to shut something such as a door or container, but a jug is made ready by adding liquid, not by shutting it
fastened means to secure something with a clip or button, which cannot be done to a jug with water from a tap

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Please ___ the door when you leave the room so the noise does not disturb the others.
distractor_rationale: cut means to use a blade to divide something — you cannot cut a door to stop noise
drop means to let something fall — a door is not dropped to shut it
burn means to set something on fire — this is nonsensical for a door in this context

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Tom had to ___ his heavy suitcase up the stairs because the lift was broken.
distractor_rationale: blow means to push air with your mouth or wind — you cannot blow a suitcase up stairs
dig means to move earth or soil with a tool — this makes no sense with a suitcase on stairs
fill means to put something inside a container — a suitcase is already packed and is being moved, not filled

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The delivery worker had to ___ the large box across the floor because it was too heavy to lift.
distractor_rationale: catch means to grab something moving through the air — a heavy box on the floor is not thrown or flying
fasten means to attach or secure something — it does not describe moving a box across a floor
bend means to curve something or your body — you cannot bend a box across the floor to move it

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The gardener used a spade to ___ a hole in the garden and then planted the small tree inside it.
distractor_rationale: deliver means to bring goods to a place — a gardener with a spade is not delivering anything
drop means to let something fall accidentally — you cannot drop a hole into the ground
break means to damage something so it comes apart — a spade is used to move earth, not to break a hole open

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Before getting on the motorbike, Anna always checks that her helmet strap is properly ___ed so that it stays secure during the ride.
distractor_rationale: fill means to put liquid or objects inside a container — a helmet strap cannot be filled to make it secure
carry means to hold and move something from one place to another — a strap is not carried; it is secured around the chin
bring means to take something to a place — a strap cannot be brought to make it stay in place on your head

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Please ___ the door when you leave — it is very cold outside.
distractor_rationale: cut means to divide with a sharp tool, which cannot be done to a door to stop cold air
drop means to let something fall, which does not apply to operating a door
dig means to move earth with a tool, which is unrelated to a door

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Tom dropped the glass and it fell to the floor and started to ___.
distractor_rationale: burn requires heat or fire, which is not present when a glass falls to the floor
bend means to curve, but glass does not bend when it hits a hard floor — it shatters
blow means to push air through something, which cannot describe what a falling glass does

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: She used a knife to ___ the bread into six equal slices for the sandwiches.
distractor_rationale: drag means to pull something along a surface, which cannot divide bread into slices
drop means to let something fall, which cannot produce equal slices of bread
carry means to transport something from one place to another, not to divide it

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The shopping bags were so heavy that Mark had to ___ them along the floor to the kitchen.
distractor_rationale: catch means to grab something moving through the air, not to pull something heavy along a floor
fill means to put something into a container until it is full, not to move a bag across a floor
blow means to push with air, which is not how a person moves heavy bags along a floor

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Anna remembered to ___ her seat belt before the car started moving on the motorway.
distractor_rationale: carry means to hold and transport something, which cannot describe securing a seat belt
dig means to move earth with a tool, which has no connection to a seat belt
drop means to let something fall, which is the opposite of securing a seat belt

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The goalkeeper managed to ___ the ball just before it crossed the goal line.
distractor_rationale: bend means to curve something, which is not what a goalkeeper does to stop a ball crossing a line
fill means to put something into a container until full, which makes no sense with a ball and goal line
deliver means to bring something to a destination, which is not the action of stopping a ball

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The worker used a shovel to ___ a deep hole in the garden before planting the new tree.
distractor_rationale: burn requires heat or fire and cannot describe making a hole with a shovel
carry means to transport something from one place to another, not to make a hole in the ground
break means to damage something into pieces, but a shovel used to make a hole in soil is digging, not breaking

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The courier arrived at the office to ___ a large package of documents that needed to be signed immediately.
distractor_rationale: carry describes the act of transporting something but does not imply the formal handover to a recipient that a courier performs
drop means to let something fall accidentally, which contradicts the professional act of a courier completing a job
bring means to take something to a place but implies the speaker's location as the destination, whereas a courier delivers to a third party's address

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Lisa carefully ___ the jug with cold water and placed it on the table so the guests could help themselves.
distractor_rationale: bent means to curve something, which cannot describe putting water into a jug
dragged means to pull something along a surface, which does not describe putting liquid into a container
fastened means to secure or close something firmly, which does not describe adding water to a jug

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The chef accidentally left the cloth too close to the gas flame and it began to ___ within seconds.
distractor_rationale: bend means to curve under pressure, which does not describe what happens to cloth exposed to a gas flame
break means to separate into pieces under force, which is not the result of cloth being near a flame
blow means to push air, which describes an action a person takes, not what happens to cloth near a flame

content.instruction: Use the word in brackets in the correct form to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Be careful not to ___ the glass when you wash it. (BREAK)

content.instruction: Use the word in brackets in the correct form to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The ___ of the heavy boxes took all morning because there were so many of them. (CARRY)

content.instruction: Use the word in brackets in the correct form to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The package arrived with a small ___ in the corner, so the shop gave us a refund. (BREAK)

content.instruction: Use the word in brackets in the correct form to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The courier made three ___ to our street before noon, so we signed for all the parcels at once. (DELIVER)

content.instruction: Use the word in brackets in the correct form to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The safety officer checked that all the ___ on the workers' protective vests were secure before they entered the site. (FASTEN)
